---
jupyter: python3
format: 
  pdf:
    echo: false
    geometry:
      - top=5mm        
      - left=5mm
      - right=5mm
      - bottom=20mm
      - headheight=35mm 
      - headsep=5mm 
      - includehead=true 
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage{multirow}
          \usepackage{graphicx}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{fancyhdr}
          \usepackage{lastpage}
          
          % Define the Page Style
          \pagestyle{fancy}
          \fancyhf{} 
          \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
          
          % Define the Header Content
          \fancyhead[C]{
            \noindent
            \bgroup
            \def\arraystretch{1.4}
            \begin{tabularx}{\textwidth}{|p{5.5cm}|p{1.8cm}|X|p{1.2cm}|p{1.8cm}|}
            \hline
            \multirow{5}{5.5cm}{
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \includegraphics[width=4.5cm]{logo} \\
                \fontsize{6pt}{7pt}\selectfont
                \textbf{CIVIL \& ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING,}\\
                SKEMPTON BUILDING, IMPERIAL COLLEGE\\
                LONDON, SOUTH KENSINGTON CAMPUS,\\
                LONDON SW7 2AZ.\\[3mm]
                \normalsize\textbf{CALCULATION SHEET}
            }
            & \textbf{Job No.} & & \textbf{Sheet} & \textbf{Ref} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \textbf{Job Title} & Steel Design Coursework & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\thepage\ of \pageref{LastPage}} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multicolumn{4}{p{\dimexpr\textwidth-5.5cm-8\tabcolsep-5\arrayrulewidth\relax}|}{
                \textbf{Subject} \newline
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \centerline{Design of Columns}
                \vspace*{1mm}
            } \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multirow{2}{*}{\textbf{Client}} & \textbf{Made by} \quad Pablo Peng & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date} \quad 30/02/2077} \\ \cline{3-5}
            & & \textbf{Checked by} \quad  & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date}} \\ \hline
            \end{tabularx}
            \egroup
          }
---
```{python}
%precision 2
# Floor area and height
hcol = 6000
lx = 8500
ly = 13500
Af1 = lx*ly/4
Af2 = lx*ly/4
Af3 = lx*ly/4
Af4 = lx*ly/4

# floor loads
gt_f = 0.7
gs_f = 2.63
gc_f = 0.5
gste_f = 0.3
gser_f = 1.4

qpar_f = 1.0
qimp_f = 4.0
qclad = 1.2

# roof loads
gt_r = 1.0
gs_r = 2.63
gc_r = 0.5
gste_r = 0.2
gser_r = 1.0

qimp_r = 1.5

# ULS floor load
Gtyp_f = gt_f + gs_f + gc_f + gste_f + gser_f
Qtyp_f = qpar_f + qimp_f + qclad
wtyp_f = 1.35*Gtyp_f + 1.5*Qtyp_f
vtyp_f = wtyp_f*(Af1 + Af2 + Af3 + Af4)/1000**2

# ULS roof load
Gtyp_r = gt_r + gs_r + gc_r + gste_r + gser_r
Qtyp_r = qimp_r
wtyp_r = 1.35*Gtyp_r + 1.5*Qtyp_r
vtyp_r = wtyp_r*(Af1 + Af2 + Af3 + Af4)/1000**2

# Moment and total load of first floor
floor = 1
num_floor = 9 - floor
if num_floor == 8:
    n =7.5
else: 
    n = num_floor
Ned = n*vtyp_f + vtyp_r
levery = 0
leverz =0
Myed = Ned*levery
Mzed = Ned*leverz
```
#

## Floor Loads
\begin{description}
    \item[]$G_{tiles}$ = `{python} gt_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{steel}$ = `{python} gste_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{cladding}$ = `{python} gc_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{slab}$ = `{python} gs_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{services}$ = `{python} gser_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$Q_{partitions}$ = `{python} qpar_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$Q_{imposed}$ = `{python} qimp_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$Q_{cladding}$ = `{python} qclad` $kN/m^2$
\end{description}

## Roof Loads
\begin{description}
    \item[]$G_{tiles}$ = `{python} gt_r` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{steel}$ = `{python} gste_r` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{cladding}$ = `{python} gc_r` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{slab}$ = `{python} gs_r` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{services}$ = `{python} gser_r` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$Q_{imposed}$ = `{python} qimp_r` $kN/m^2$
\end{description}

## ULS floor uniform distributed load
\begin{description}
    \item[]Typical dead load, $G_{typ}$ = $G_{tiles} + G_{steel} + G_{cladding} + G_{slab} + G_{services}$ = `{python} Gtyp_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]Typical imposed load, $Q_{typ}$ = $Q_{partitions} + Q_{imposed} + Q_{cladding}$ = `{python} Qtyp_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]Typical load, $W_{typ}$ = $1.35\cdot G_{typ} + 1.5 \cdot Q_{typ}$ = `{python} wtyp_f` $kN/m^2$
\end{description}

<pagebreak>

#

## ULS roof uniform distributed load
\begin{description}
    \item[]Typical dead load, $G_{typ}$ = $G_{tiles} + G_{steel} + G_{cladding} + G_{slab} + G_{services}$ = `{python} Gtyp_r` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]Typical imposed load, $Q_{typ}$ = $Q_{imposed}$ = `{python} Qtyp_r` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]Typical load, $W_{typ}$ = $1.35\cdot G_{typ} + 1.5 \cdot Q_{typ}$ = `{python} wtyp_r` $kN/m^2$
\end{description}

## Load areas
\begin{description}
    \item[]Column height, $h_{col}$ = `{python} hcol` $mm$
    \item[]Horizontal distance, $L_{x}$ = `{python} lx` $mm$
    \item[]Horizontal distance, $L_{y}$ = `{python} ly` $mm$
    \item[]Floor area, $A_{f}$ = $A_1 + A_2 + A_3 + A_4$ = `{python} Af1 + Af2 + Af3 + Af4` $mm^2$
\end{description}

## Total Axial load and momenton column (half the load for first floor)
\begin{description}
    \item[]Number of floors above, n = `{python} num_floor`
    \item[]Single floor load, $V_{typ,f}$ = $W_{typ,f}\cdot A_{f}$ = `{python} vtyp_f` kN
    \item[]Roof load, $V_{typ,r}$ = $W_{typ,r}\cdot A_{f}$ = `{python} vtyp_r` kN
    \item[]Vertical load on column, $V_{ed1}$ = $7.5\cdot V_{typ,f} + V_{typ,r}$ = `{python} Ned` kN
    \item[]Lever arm about y-axis, $L_{y}$ = `{python} levery` m
    \item[]Lever arm about z-axis, $L_{x}$ = `{python} leverz` m
    \item[]Moment about y-axis, $M_{y}$ = $V_{ed1}\cdot \frac{L_{y}}{2}$ = `{python} Myed` kNm
    \item[]Moment about z-axis, $M_{z}$ = $V_{ed1}\cdot \frac{L_{x}}{2}$ = `{python} Mzed` kNm
\end{description}


```{python}
#material
fu= 500.0
fy = 355.0
E = 210000
G = 81000

import pandas as pd
df_all = pd.read_csv('section_mm.csv')
if df_all.columns[0] == 'Unnamed: 0':
  df_all = df_all.rename(columns = {'Unnamed: 0' : 'Section Profile'})

section = '356 x 406x 551'
df = df_all[df_all['Section Profile'] == section]
#section
h = float(df['h (mm)'].iloc[0])
b = float(df['b (mm)'].iloc[0])
tw = float(df['tw (mm)'].iloc[0])
tf = float(df['tf (mm)'].iloc[0])
d = float(df['d (mm)'].iloc[0])
r= float(df['r(mm)'].iloc[0])
A = float(df['A (mm^2)'].iloc[0])
Iy = float(df['Iy (mm^4)'].iloc[0])
Iz = float(df['Iz (mm^4)'].iloc[0])
Ry = float(df['Ry (mm)'].iloc[0])
Rz = float(df['Rz (mm)'].iloc[0])
Wey = float(df['W_el,y (mm^3)'].iloc[0])
Wez = float(df['W_el,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0])
Wpy = float(df['W_pl,y (mm3)'].iloc[0])
Wpz = float(df['W_pl,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0])
Iw = float(df['I_w (mm^6)'].iloc[0])
It = float(df['I_T (mm^4)'].iloc[0])

ratio = h/b
if ratio > 1.2:
  if tf <= 40:
    ay = 0.21
    az = 0.34
  else:
    ay = 0.34
    az = 0.49
else:
  if tf <= 100:
    ay = 0.34
    az = 0.49
  else:
    ay = 0.70
    az = 0.70
```

<pagebreak>

#  

## Effective length of column about y-y

\begin{description}
  \item[]Ultimate strength: $f_{u}$ = `{python} fu` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Yield strength: $f_{y}$ = `{python} fy` $N \cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Young modulus: E = `{python} E` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Shear modulus: G = `{python} G` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$
\end{description}

<pagebreak>

#

## Section Properties of 356 x 406 x 634

\begin{description}
  \item[]Overall depth, h = `{python} h` mm  
  \item[]Width of section, b = `{python} b` mm  
  \item[]Web thickness, $t_{w}$ = `{python} tw`  mm  
  \item[]Flange thickness, $t_{f}$ = `{python} tf` mm  
  \item[]Depth between fillet, $h_{w}$ = `{python} d` mm  
  \item[]Root radius, r = `{python} r` mm  
  \item[]Area of section, $A$ = `{python} A`  $mm^{2}$   
  \item[]2nd moment area Major axis, $I_{y}$ = `{python} Iy` $mm^{4}$  
  \item[]2nd moment area minor axis, $I_{z}$ = `{python} Iz` $mm^{4}$  
  \item[]Radius of gyration major axis $i_{y}$ = `{python} Ry` mm  
  \item[]Radius of gyration minor axis, $i_{z}$ = `{python} Rz` mm  
  \item[]Elastic modulus major axis, $W_{el,y}$ = `{python} Wey` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[]Elastic modulus minor axis, $W_{el,z}$ = `{python} Wez` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[]Plastic modulus major axis, $W_{pl,y}$ = `{python} Wpy` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[]Plastic modulus minor axis, $W_{pl,z}$ = `{python} Wpz` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[]Warping Constant, $I_{w}$ = `{python} Iw` $mm^{6}$  
  \item[]Torsional constant, $I_{t}$ = `{python} It` $mm^{4}$  
\end{description}

```{python}
import math
ep = 235/fy
crf = 9*ep
crw = 28*ep
cw = d
c1 = cw/tw
cf = (b-tw-2*r)/2
c2 = cf/tf
```

{{<pagebreak>}}

#

## Section Classification

\begin{description}
  \item[]$\epsilon$ = $\sqrt{\frac{235}{f_{y}}}$ = `{python} ep` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 5.1)
  \item[]Web Classification: 
  \item[]$c_{w}$ = $\frac{(b-t_{w}-2\cdot r)}{2}$ = `{python} cw` 
  \item[]$\frac{c_{w}}{t_{w}}$ = `{python} c1`
  \item[]Class 1 criteria $\leq$ $28\epsilon$ = `{python} crw` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Table 7.3)
  \item[]Flange Classification: 
  \item[]$c_{f}$ = Depth between filet = `{python} cf` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Table 7.4)
  \item[]$\frac{c_{f}}{t_{f}}$ = `{python} c2` 
  \item[]Class 1 criteria $\leq$ $9\epsilon$ = `{python} crf` 
  \item[]Since $\frac{c_{w}}{t_{w}}$ < $28\epsilon$ and $\frac{c_{f}}{t_{f}}$ < $9\epsilon$, the section belongs to class 1  
\end{description}   

```{python}
def buckle (Lcr, I, E, A, fy, a):
  Ncr = (math.pi**2 * E * I) / ((Lcr)**2)*1e-3
  lam = math.sqrt(( A*fy / (Ncr*1e3) ))
  phi = 0.5*(1 + a*(lam - 0.2) + lam**2)
  chi = min(1/(phi + math.sqrt(phi**2 - lam**2)),1)
  return Ncr, lam, ratio, phi, chi
span = hcol
Lcr = hcol/2
Ncry, lamy, ratio, phiy, chiy = buckle(Lcr, Iy, E, A, fy, ay)
Ncrz, lamz, ratio, phiz, chiz = buckle(Lcr, Iz, E, A, fy, az)

gamma1 = 1.0
chi = min([chiy,chiz])
Nrd = chi*A*fy/gamma1/1000

if Nrd > Ned:
  symbol = '>'
  output = 'safe in resisting axial load'
else:
  symbol = '<'
  output = 'Not safe in resisting axial load'
```

## Buckling resistance about y-y axis

\begin{description}
  \item[]span = `{python} span` mm
  \item[]$L_{cr,y}$ = $\frac{span}{2}$ = `{python} Lcr` mm
  \item[]$N_{cr,y}$ = $\frac{\pi^{2}\cdot E\cdot I_{y}}{{L_{cr,y}}^{2}}$ = `{python} Ncry` kN
  \item[]$\lambda_{y}$ =  $\sqrt{\frac{N_{Rk}}{N_{cr,y}}}$ = $\sqrt{\frac{A\cdot f_{y}}{N_{cr,y}}}$ = `{python} lamy` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.69)
  \item[]$\frac{h}{b}$ = `{python} ratio` < 1.2
  \item[]$t_{f}$ = `{python} tf` mm < 100~mm
  \item[]Use buckling curve B,  $\alpha_{y}$ = `{python} ay` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Table 8.2)
  \item[]$\phi_{y}$ = $0.5 \cdot (1 + \alpha_{y} \cdot (\lambda_{y} - 0.2) + {\lambda_{y}}^{2})$ = `{python} phiy` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.74)
  \item[]$\chi_{y}$ = $\frac{1}{\phi_{y} + \sqrt{{\phi_{y}}^{2} - {\lambda_{y}}^{2}}}$ = `{python} chiy` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.73)
\end{description}

{{<pagebreak>}}

#

## Buckling resistance about z-z axis
\begin{description}
  \item[]$L_{cr,z}$ = $\frac{span}{2}$ = `{python} Lcr` mm
  \item[]$N_{cr,z}$ = $\frac{\pi^{2}\cdot E\cdot I_{z}}{{L_{cr,z}}^{2}}$ = `{python} Ncrz` kN
  \item[]$\lambda_{z}$ = $\sqrt{\frac{N_{Rk}}{N_{cr,z}}}$ = $\sqrt{\frac{A\cdot f_{y}}{N_{cr,z}}}$ = `{python} lamz` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.69)
  \item[]$\frac{h}{b}$ = `{python} ratio` < 1.2
  \item[]$t_{f}$ = `{python} tf` mm < 100~mm
  \item[]Use buckling curve C,  $\alpha_{z}$ = `{python} az` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Table 8.2)
  \item[]$\phi_{z}$ = $0.5 \cdot (1 + \alpha_{z} \cdot (\lambda_{z} - 0.2) + {\lambda_{z}}^{2})$ = `{python} phiz` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.74)
  \item[]$\chi_{z}$ = $\frac{1}{\phi_{z} + \sqrt{{\phi_{z}}^{2} - {\lambda_{z}}^{2}}}$ = `{python} chiz` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.73)
  
\end{description}
  
## Buckling resistance
\begin{description}
  \item[]$\chi$ = min($\chi_{y},\chi_{z}$) = `{python} chi`
  \item[]$N_{RD}$ = $\chi\cdot A\cdot f_{y}$ = `{python} Nrd` kN --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.68)
  \item[]Maximum axial compressive force is `{python} Ned` kN.
  \item[]Since $N_{RD}$ `{python} symbol` $N_{ED}$, `{python} output`
\end{description}

```{python}
Lcr = hcol
Nrk = fy*A**1e-3
Mcr = ((math.pi**2 * E * Iz) / Lcr**2) * (Iw/Iz + (G * It * (Lcr)**2)/ (math.pi**2 * E * It)) **1e-6

if ratio > 1.2:
    if tf <= 40:
        alt = min(0.12*math.sqrt(Wey/Wez), 0.34)
    else:
        alt = min(0.16*math.sqrt(Wey/Wez), 0.49)
else:
    alt = min(0.16*math.sqrt(Wey/Wez), 0.49)

phi_lt = 0.5*(1 + alt*(min(lamy,lamz) - 0.2) + min(lamy,lamz)**2)
chi_lt = min(1/(phi_lt + math.sqrt(phi_lt**2 - min(lamy,lamz)**2)),1)

Myrk = Wpy*fy*1e-6
Mzrk = Wpz*fy*1e-6
Mbrd = chi_lt*Myrk*1e-6/gamma1
Mcrdz = Mzrk*1e-6/gamma1

if Mbrd > Myed and Mcrdz > Mzed:
    symbolm = '>'
    outputm = 'safe in resisting bending'
else:
    symbolm = '<'
    outputm = 'not safe in resisting bending'

int_ratio = Ned/Nrk + Myed/Mbrd + Mzed/Mcrdz
if int_ratio < 1.0:
    symboli = '<'
    outputi = 'safe in resisting interaction'
else:
    symboli = '>'
    outputi = 'not safe in resisting interaction'
```

## Lateral torsional buckling check

\begin{description}
    \item[]Critical moment, $M_{cr}$= $\frac{\pi^{2} \cdot E \cdot I_{z}}{L_{cr}^{2}} \cdot \left( \frac{I_{w}}{I_{z}} + \frac{G \cdot I_{t} \cdot L_{cr}^{2}}{\pi^{2} \cdot E \cdot I_{t}} \right)$ = `{python} Mcr` kNm
    \item[]For $\frac{h}{b}$ > 1.2 and $t_{f}$ <= 40 mm, $\alpha_{LT}$ = min($0.12\cdot \sqrt{\frac{W_{el,y}}{W_{el,z}}}$, 0.34) = `{python} alt` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Table 8.5)
    \item[]$\phi_{LT}$ = $0.5\cdot(1 + alt\cdot(min(\lambda_y,\lambda_z) - 0.2) + min(\lambda_y,\lambda_z)^{2})$ = `{python} phi_lt` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.82)
    \item[]$\chi_{LT}$ = $min(\frac{1}{\phi_{LT} + \sqrt{\phi_{LT}^{2} - min(\lambda_y,\lambda_z)^{2}}},1)$ = `{python} chi_lt` --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.81)
    \item[]Moment resistance, $M_{brd}$ = $\frac{\chi_{LT} \cdot W_{pl,y} \cdot f_{y}}{\gamma_1}$ = `{python} Mbrd` kNm --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.79)
    \item[]Moment resistance about z-axis, $M_{cr,dz}$ = $\frac{W_{pl,z} \cdot f_{y}}{\gamma_1}$ = `{python} Mcrdz` kNm --- (EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.20)
    \item[] Since $M_{brd}$ `{python} symbolm` $M_{y,ed}$ and $M_{cr,dz}$ `{python} symbolm` $M_{z,ed}$, `{python} outputm`
\end{description}
<pagebreak>

#

## Axial and bending interaction check

\begin{description}
    \item[]Assume simple construction and refer to EN 1993-1-1 :2022, Eq 8.59 
    \item[]Interaction ratio = $\frac{N_{ed}}{N_{rk}} + \frac{M_{y}}{M_{brd}} + \frac{M_{z}}{M_{cr,dz}}$ = `{python} int_ratio`
    \item[]Since interaction ratio `{python} symboli` 1.0, the section is `{python} outputi`
\end{description}
